meta.program: ATG (Athletic Truth Group / Ben Patrick — Knees Over Toes)
meta.structure: 3 blocks. Each block = 4 weeks (Week 1-3 Build, Week 4 Deload). Each week = 3 sessions: Day 1, Day 2, Day 3.
meta.tempo_notation: 4 digits = eccentric/bottom-pause/concentric/top-pause seconds (e.g. 30X1 = 3s down, 0s pause, eXplosive up, 1s hold). '-' = not a tempo lift (sled, sprint, jump, hold-based).
meta.progression: Wave-loading within a session: set 1 is bodyweight/light, each following set adds load or reps only if the prior set was pain-free with full range and good form. Week over week, aim to add 1 rep or 1 load increment on the top working set. Exercise selection is intentionally identical across all 3 blocks — progression comes from logged load/reps over time, not from new exercises each block.
meta.deload_rule: Week 4 of every block: cut total sets ~40%, keep loads moderate, no new PRs. Use the extra recovery to retest 2-3 entries from atg_standards instead of pushing load.
meta.note_on_distance_time_exercises: A few rows (backward sled/sprint, jumps, holds) use distance/time in the reps field instead of a rep count (e.g. '40m', '15s'). The standard double-progression rep-range suggestion logic does not apply cleanly to these — treat them as logged-but-not-auto-suggested, or skip the suggestion calculation when reps is non-numeric.
meta.source: Derived from Ben Patrick's published ATG material ("The Beginner's ATG Lifting Program", ATG Standards) and the companion ATG_Training_Program_Gabriele.docx / ATG_Training_Tracker_Gabriele.xlsx in this folder.

Day 1: Backward Sled Drag or Backward Walk
Ankle Ladder (Patrick Step / Poliquin Step Up / Peterson Step — rotate weekly)
Knuckles for balance only, no hand assistance on load
Set 1 bodyweight, add DB load each set if pain-free
Knees to chest, or 15s L-sit hold
Day 2: Heavier than Day 1 — this is the heavy backward day
Machine, DB/KB on thigh, or bent-knee bodyweight KOT raise
Elbows in front of body, knees track over toes
Seated against wall to start; progress to free-standing, then loaded
Day 3: Push the pace, not just the load
ATG Split Squat — moderate load, explosive up
Pause 1s at bottom, drive up with intent
Box limits range early on; lower it over time
Pallof Press or Rotational Med Ball Throw
Anti-rotation core, ties lower body power into the trunk

atg_standards.name: Sissy Squat
atg_standards.target: 20 reps bodyweight, shins parallel to floor

atg_standards.name: Hanging Leg Raise
atg_standards.target: Toes to bar, 10 reps, 2s controlled eccentric
